How Prediction Markets and Crypto Firms Steamrolled a Watchdog Agency

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission is a small federal agency, but one with big responsibilities. Part of its job is to protect the millions of Americans who buy crypto or bet on prediction markets.

The Trump family is deeply invested in those industries. The Trumps have generated enormous wealth from cryptocurrency and have positioned themselves to haul in profits from prediction markets as well.
But in the past 16 months, the C.F.T.C. has shrunk its work force, purged career officials, sharply curtailed crypto enforcement and helped out prediction markets at virtually every turn.

We found that some senior career officials who enforced laws involving cryptocurrencies or raised questions about prediction market companies were put on leave, barred from the office and placed under investigation.

The agency’s chairman, appointed by Trump, told us that the C.F.T.C. will still police fraud, insider trading and other abuses. He is now the sole commissioner because the president left the other board seats vacant.
Jason Andrew for The New York Times
